title: Pyrus × bollwylleriana
taxonomy: Pyrus × bollwylleriana is a hybrid cultivar of the Pyrus species. It belongs to the family Rosaceae. Within the genus Pyrus, it is classified under the Rosoideae subfamily. This particular hybrid is valued for its ornamental and edible properties, including its fruit and foliage. As a cultivated variety, its classification is primarily used for identification and propagation purposes.

Here's a paragraph on the common pear (Pyrus communis):

The common pear (Pyrus communis) is a deciduous tree species in the family Rosaceae, known for its edible fruit and traditional medicinal uses. The fruit of the common pear is rich in antioxidants and has been associated with various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anti-cancer properties. The bioactive constituents of the common pear include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with the common pear include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
